lede: A look at June 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Meryl Streep and Cyndi Lauper, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United States
events.desc: The United States conducts airstrikes on three Iranian nuclear sites in Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan.

events.title: June 2022 Afghanistan earthquake
events.desc: An earthquake occurs in eastern Afghanistan resulting in over 1,000 deaths.

events.title: Paraguay
events.desc: Paraguayan President Fernando Lugo is removed from office by impeachment and succeeded by Federico Franco.

events.title: Washington Metro
events.desc: A Washington D.C Metro train traveling southbound near Fort Totten station collides into another train waiting to enter the station. Nine people are killed in the collision (eight passengers and the train operator) and at least 80 others are injured.

events.title: Liberal Party (UK)
events.desc: Former Liberal Party leader Jeremy Thorpe was acquitted of conspiracy to murder Norman Scott, who had accused Thorpe of having a relationship with him.

events.title: Buddhism in Vietnam
events.desc: Vietnamese Buddhist activist leader Thích Trí Quang was arrested as the military junta of Nguyen Cao Ky crushed the Buddhist Uprising.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: France is forced to sign the Second Compiègne armistice with Germany, in the same railroad car in which the Germans signed the Armistice in 1918.

events.title: George V
events.desc: George V and Mary of Teck are crowned King and Queen of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland.

events.title: War of 1812
events.desc: War of 1812: After learning of American plans for a surprise attack on Beaver Dams in Ontario, Laura Secord sets out on a thirty kilometres (19 mi) journey on foot to warn Lieutenant James FitzGibbon.

events.title: 1812 French declaration of war on Russia
events.desc: France declares war on Russia, starting Napoleon's invasion.

deaths.text: David O. Selznick, American screenwriter and producer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Alban, first recorded Martyr in Britain (commemoration, Anglicanism)
Christian feast day: Blessed Pope Innocent V
Christian feast day: Eusebius of Samosata (Eastern Orthodox Church)
